{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 2,
    "R1.4": 3,
    "R1.7": 1,
    "RX.1": 4,
    "RX.3": 3,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 5
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The agent identifies one conceptual issue (the IV claim contradicts the unconfoundedness assumption) and proposes a reframing, but does not fully address the problem.",
    "R1.4": "The agent notes that 'heterogeneity as model-family entropy' is under-specified and likely confounded with capability, but does not provide a clear solution to this issue.",
    "R1.7": "The agent flags no missing or wrong citations, but the paper could benefit from more precise citation of prior work in the causal-inference treatment of multi-agent aggregation.",
    "RX.1": "I have not verified the specific numbers (4,200 runs, +1.8pp, CI bounds) against any underlying data; I assume they are reported faithfully.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers mostly within scope with one drift (the IV sketch), but the agent could provide more clarity on how this relates to the rest of the paper.",
    "RX.4": "No regressions; the agent identifies harmful patterns in the original, specifically the potential for joint-capability confounding.",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, but the agent could provide more explicit citations to support the claims made."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.8,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.6,
    "RX.3": 0.8,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.95
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The agent identifies one conceptual issue (the IV claim contradicts the unconfoundedness assumption) and proposes a reframing, but does not fully address the problem.
rationale.R1.4: The agent notes that 'heterogeneity as model-family entropy' is under-specified and likely confounded with capability, but does not provide a clear solution to this issue.
rationale.R1.7: The agent flags no missing or wrong citations, but the paper could benefit from more precise citation of prior work in the causal-inference treatment of multi-agent aggregation.
rationale.RX.1: I have not verified the specific numbers (4,200 runs, +1.8pp, CI bounds) against any underlying data; I assume they are reported faithfully.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers mostly within scope with one drift (the IV sketch), but the agent could provide more clarity on how this relates to the rest of the paper.
rationale.RX.4: No regressions; the agent identifies harmful patterns in the original, specifically the potential for joint-capability confounding.
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, but the agent could provide more explicit citations to support the claims made.
